stages.title: Unified preflight

tasks.action: Clone master, record HEAD SHA, verify VF-01..VF-04 still hold. If any verified finding has changed (e.g. parity tooling now exists), amend this plan before executing further.
acceptance: All VF findings reconfirmed or plan amended with recorded rationale.

tasks.action: Commit this unified plan to EAFIX_auth_docs/00_doc_control_and_authority/ and register it in doc_authority.json as the execution-ordering authority governing the five source plans.
acceptance: Plan committed; doc_authority.json updated; source plans marked governed-by.

tasks.action: Commit the populated 34-module vNext catalog (modules[] currently empty on master despite module_count 34; populated version exists in project knowledge). This is a known commit gap, not an open lock decision, and is a precondition for STAGE-6 WS1 and the naming plan's precondition 3.1.
acceptance: Claude_gen_atomic_module_catalog_vNext.json on master contains 34 populated module records matching LOCKED_PASS_1.

stages.gate_out: Plan ratified and committed; repo baseline recorded; vNext catalog populated on master.

stages.title: Registry determinism and validation remediation

explicit_false_positive.finding: Add main to the registry workflow filters
explicit_false_positive.reason: The repository default branch is master and the workflow already targets master and develop.

environmental_blocker.name: GitHub Actions billing/account lock
environmental_blocker.effect: Checks may fail before any job starts.
environmental_blocker.rule: Do not count startup-blocked checks as passing or as code failures. Complete local validation, but do not merge the remediation until CI can execute.

confirmed_defects.title: Manifest paths are platform-dependent
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Use repository-relative POSIX paths with Path.as_posix().

confirmed_defects.title: Check mode compares only registry snapshots
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Render and compare every managed generated artifact in --check mode without writing.

confirmed_defects.title: Module process_step_ids are not foreign-key validated
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Validate every module process_step_id against the process registry.

confirmed_defects.title: Writer is incompatible with declared Python 3.9 support
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Replace Path.write_text(..., newline=...) with Python 3.9-compatible stable file writing.

confirmed_defects.title: Generated parity failure is advisory
confirmed_defects.required_fix: Make structural validation and deterministic parity blocking; keep only governance review findings advisory.

implementation_architecture.pattern: pure render, then compare or write

components.name: render_expected_outputs
components.contract: Return a deterministic mapping of repository-relative POSIX paths to exact UTF-8 bytes for every managed generated artifact.

components.name: compare_expected_outputs
components.contract: Compare every expected artifact against disk, report missing/different outputs, and perform no writes.

components.name: write_expected_outputs
components.contract: Write the exact rendered bytes using UTF-8 and LF newlines through Python 3.9-compatible APIs.

components.name: build_manifest
components.contract: Hash authored registry inputs and all generated outputs except the manifest itself; store only POSIX path keys.

components.name: run
components.contract: Load, validate, render, compare or write, classify blocking errors versus advisory findings, print a deterministic summary, and return an appropriate exit code.

tasks.action: Create a clean branch from current master.
acceptance: Working tree is clean.
Branch contains merge commit 5b28d4ac4d695ea5ac319a344f97ce9a7f32ba1e or a later verified master commit.

tasks.action: Read all required routing and authority references.
evidence: List reviewed files in the PR description.
Confirm no authority-cutover file will be modified.

tasks.action: Capture baseline behavior.
evidence: Whether two normal builds modify registry_build_manifest.json

tasks.action: Implement one render function for all managed outputs.
requirements: Render snapshots, graph, reports, Mermaid, Markdown views, and manifest in memory.
Build the manifest last.
Do not hash the manifest itself.
Use stable sorted JSON with a trailing newline.
Use POSIX repository-relative path keys.

tasks.action: Implement Python 3.9-compatible file output.
tasks.required_pattern: Use Path.open('w', encoding='utf-8', newline='\n') or binary writes.
tasks.prohibited_pattern: Do not pass newline to Path.write_text.

tasks.action: Make write and check modes consume the same rendered output mapping.
acceptance: No generated artifact has a separate check-mode logic path.
Write mode writes exactly what check mode expects.

tasks.action: Exclude the manifest from its output_hashes.
Normalize all input_hashes and output_hashes keys with as_posix().
Add an internal manifest verifier.
requirements: Every listed input path exists and matches its recorded hash.
Every listed output path exists and matches its recorded hash.
registry_build_manifest.json is not listed as an output.
No key contains a backslash.

tasks.action: Compare every managed generated artifact under --check.
acceptance: --check writes nothing.
--check returns zero only when every managed output is current.
Tampering any managed output returns nonzero.

tasks.action: Separate blocking errors from advisory governance findings.
tasks.rule: --report-only may prevent advisory findings from failing the command, but must not hide blocking errors.

tasks.action: Validate module process_step_ids against process registry IDs.
tasks.error_contract: Include registry name, record_id, field, and invalid target ID.

tasks.action: Audit every schema-declared ID-reference field against validate_refs.
tasks.rule: Add validator coverage only for declared relationships. Do not invent new relationships or populate missing data.

phases.name: Add regression and mutation tests
test_rules: Use temporary directories or patched roots.
Never leave committed files modified after a test.
Tests must pass on Windows and Linux.
Python 3.9 CI execution is the authoritative compatibility proof.

tasks.action: Add a Python matrix.
Make integrity and parity blocking.
tasks.required_change: Remove continue-on-error from the structural validation and --check step.

tasks.action: Keep governance reports advisory and upload them with if: always().
Preserve branch filters master and develop.
tasks.prohibited_change: Do not add main solely because of the incorrect review comment.

recommended_workflow_sequence: Run git diff --exit-code for generated directories

acceptance: First command writes only expected generated changes.
Second command reports errors=0 and changed=0.
No authored JSONL record changes unless separately justified.
No routing or authority files change.

tasks.action: Prove two-build determinism.
acceptance: The second build creates no diff.
The manifest remains byte-identical.

tasks.action: Review all generated diffs.

phases.name: Full local validation
mutation_tests: Tamper each output class and prove --check fails without rewriting.
Inject an invalid module process_step_id in a temporary fixture and prove reference validation fails.
Restore clean state and prove all validation passes.

commands: git commit -m "fix(registry): remediate deterministic validation defects"

tasks.action: Open a PR against master.
required_pr_sections: Governance boundary and out-of-scope authority cutover

tasks.action: Document disposition of PR #74 review comments.

dispositions.result: rejected because master is the default branch

tasks.action: Resolve the GitHub Actions billing/account lock.

tasks.rule: Skipped, neutral, startup-blocked, or account-locked checks are not passing evidence.

merge_conditions: All blocking checks execute and pass
No unresolved P1/P2 review thread on remediation code
Two consecutive builds produce no diff
PR description matches the final state

change: Add regression and mutation coverage for every confirmed defect

acceptance_criteria: 291 records across 10 registries validate.
Schema, ID, reference, and authority errors are zero.
Every managed generated artifact is checked by --check.
--check writes no files.
The manifest contains no self-hash.
Manifest paths use forward slashes.
Every manifest hash verifies.
Module process_step_ids are validated.
The suite passes on Python 3.9 and 3.11.
Two consecutive builds produce no Git diff.
Tampering any managed generated artifact causes --check to fail.
Structural validation and generated parity block CI.
The 25 review items and two orphan items remain visible unless separately resolved.
No routing or authority cutover occurs.
GitHub checks execute after the account lock is removed.
Doc ID Validation and title lint pass.
evidence_required: Explicit confirmation that authority cutover was not performed

fail_closed_conditions: Unexpected authored registry changes
Record count changes without approved source changes
Output variance between Python 3.9 and 3.11
Second build produces a diff
Check mode writes files
Routing or authority files appear in the diff
Review or orphan items disappear without evidence
CI cannot execute
response: Revert only the remediation branch changes.
Classify the cause as generator logic, source drift, platform variance, or environmental CI blockage.
Open a focused issue for out-of-scope governance or data conflicts.

stages.gate_out: All acceptance criteria of EAFIX-REGISTRY-POST-MERGE-REMEDIATION-001 satisfied and merged to master: deterministic two-build no-diff, complete --check coverage, Python 3.9/3.11 green, blocking CI integrity checks, no authority cutover performed.

stages.title: Authority parity system (fills missing predecessor EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001)
stages.rationale: The burndown plan requires a parity report and frozen baseline so that 'resolved' has a machine-checkable meaning (its predecessor_dependency). Neither the plan nor its artifacts exist on master (VF-03). This stage delivers exactly the deliverables the burndown plan's gate BURN-001 checks for; deliverable names are taken verbatim from the burndown plan's scope and commands, not invented.

tasks.action: Author EAFIX_auth_docs/01_canonical_registries/parity_authority_map.json declaring, per cutover-track registry (module, process, contract, artifact), which legacy document is the parity authority.
constraints: Derive the map from eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json routes; do not invent authority assignments.
acceptance: Every cutover-track registry has exactly one declared parity authority with a recorded routing justification.

tasks.action: Implement tools/registries/authority_projection.py: deterministic adapters projecting each legacy authority into the registry record shape (normalized keys, join keys, field mapping).
acceptance: Projection is deterministic (two runs, no diff).
Every adapter has unit tests in tests/registries/.

tasks.action: Implement tools/registries/validate_authority_parity.py --check: compare projected legacy authorities against authored registries, emitting a parity report with per-record verdicts and counts new_divergences and stale_baseline_entries.
acceptance: Non-zero exit on new_divergences>0 or stale_baseline_entries>0 unless in --baseline mode.
Report is a managed generated artifact covered by build --check.

tasks.action: Freeze the initial divergence baseline: EAFIX_auth_docs/01_canonical_registries/registry_authority_parity_baseline.json enumerating every current divergence with a parity_id. The baseline may only shrink thereafter, or be re-derived under governed exception per burndown PHASE-02.
acceptance: Baseline committed; validate_authority_parity.py --check green (new_divergences=0, stale_baseline_entries=0).
Baseline entry count recorded in evidence.

tasks.action: Wire the parity check into .github/workflows/registry-validation.yml as blocking, preserving STAGE-1 advisory/blocking separation.
acceptance: CI blocks on new divergences; governance reports remain advisory.

stages.gate_out: BURN-001's command set passes on master: build --check green, validate_authority_parity.py --check green, tests green, registry_index unchanged (canonical_candidate, approved_for_cutover false). The burndown plan's status flips from blocked_on_predecessor to ready.

stages.title: Conflict and divergence burn-down
stages.gate_in: STAGE-1 and STAGE-2 merged with acceptance satisfied. This replaces the original predecessor gate on EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001, delivered here as STAGE-2.

in_scope: EAFIX_auth_docs/01_canonical_registries/registry_authority_parity_baseline.json (shrink only, or governed re-derivation in PHASE-02)
out_of_scope: Registry schema changes (unrepresentable verdicts are EAFIX-REGISTRY-COVERAGE-001, STEP-06)
Fixing code-level integration defects discovered during contract burn-down

explicit_non_goal.name: Making parity green by copying
explicit_non_goal.reason: A registry record that agrees with the authority because it was copied from the authority carries zero independent evidentiary weight. It cannot corroborate the authority, cannot detect authority staleness, and cannot justify cutover — because cutting over to a copy replaces a document with a worse-formatted version of the same document. Every resolution must trace to a primary source, or be explicitly and visibly marked as authority-derived at reduced confidence.
explicit_non_goal.detection: PHASE-07 audits the ratio of resolutions carrying E1/E2 primary evidence versus E3-only. A burn-down that is overwhelmingly E3-only did not investigate; it transcribed.

environmental_blocker.name: GitHub Actions billing/account lock
environmental_blocker.effect: Checks may fail before any job starts.
environmental_blocker.rule: Do not count startup-blocked checks as passing. Complete local validation, but do not merge any batch until CI can execute.

confirmed_open_findings.title: 22 contract records flagged conflicted / needs_review
confirmed_open_findings.why_hardest: Contracts are where coupling lives. A contract conflict means two modules disagree about their interface. The old documents papered over that; the registry surfaced it. Some fraction of these are registry defects and some are real integration defects in the system itself.
confirmed_open_findings.target: needs_review = 0 across contract_registry; every conflict either resolved with evidence or converted to a code issue with the registry recording the true conflicting state

confirmed_open_findings.title: Parity baseline non-empty for cutover-track registries
confirmed_open_findings.why_hardest: The baseline is the only machine-checkable definition of 'the registries disagree with reality'. It is the burn-down ledger. Its size at the start of this plan is unknown until PHASE-00 snapshots it.
confirmed_open_findings.target: baseline empty for module, process, contract, artifact registries

confirmed_open_findings.title: Stub module records with confidence medium and empty responsibilities / process_step_ids
confirmed_open_findings.source: module_registry.jsonl (known: R3_CORRELATION_GUARD, U1_DASHBOARD_BACKEND, U2_GUI_GATEWAY; full list determined in PHASE-00)
confirmed_open_findings.note: 34/34 modules are populated but several are shells. The registry could not invent facts that were never written down, so it flagged the hole. Filling the hole requires finding the facts, not fabricating them.
confirmed_open_findings.target: zero records with empty responsibilities or empty process_step_ids where the module has an implementation; modules with no implementation recorded explicitly as planned status

confirmed_open_findings.title: 25 governance review items and 2 orphan classifications
confirmed_open_findings.source: registry_conflict_queue.jsonl; orphan report (includes SK1_PLUGIN_INTERFACE and SK2_IDEMPOTENCY)
confirmed_open_findings.target: conflict queue empty; both orphans dispositioned with a decision_registry record

confirmed_open_findings.title: Possible dangling module.process_step_id references
confirmed_open_findings.source: AMD-REG-001 preflight probe result from the remediation plan
confirmed_open_findings.conditional: Applies only if the remediation plan's PHASE-00 probe found a non-empty difference and baselined those errors.
confirmed_open_findings.target: baselined foreign_reference_errors for module.process_step_ids reduced to zero; the temporary exemption added by AMD-REG-001 removed from the remediation plan's required_results

evidence_protocol.principle: A resolution is an assertion about the system. Every assertion carries its evidence class in the record's source_refs. The registry's value is that its facts are traceable; a resolution without a trace destroys that value for the record it touches.

evidence_classes.description: Implementation source: file path plus commit SHA plus the specific symbol, function, message type, or field observed.
A concrete non-code artifact in the repository: manifest, config file, schema, CI definition, .mqh header, message contract file.
A canonical catalog asserts the fact.
evidence_classes.rule: E3 alone never justifies confidence high. A record resolved on E3 alone is tagged derived_from_authority_only and remains visible as such. This is the single most important rule in this plan.

evidence_classes.description: Superseded documents, git history, prior commits, retired manifests.
evidence_classes.rule: Never sufficient alone. May corroborate E1/E2 or explain how a divergence arose.

evidence_classes.description: A recorded human architectural decision by the repository owner.
evidence_classes.strength: authoritative for decisions, not for facts
evidence_classes.rule: Requires a decision_registry record with rationale. Valid for 'what shall be', invalid for 'what is'.

resolution_rules: A registry_defect resolution SHOULD carry at least one E1 or E2 reference.

Where no E1/E2 evidence exists and the fact is asserted only by the authority, the resolution is permitted but MUST set confidence to medium and MUST tag the record derived_from_authority_only. Such records are visible in the completeness report and are a known cutover consideration, not a hidden one.

Where the registry and the authority disagree AND primary evidence supports the registry, the disposition is authority_stale_suspected. Record it, open a governance issue, and leave the divergence baselined. This plan does not edit authority documents.

Where primary evidence contradicts BOTH the registry and the authority, that is the most valuable finding in the burn-down. Record it, correct the registry to match primary evidence, open a governance issue for the authority, and leave the resulting parity divergence baselined with disposition authority_stale_suspected.
Never delete a record to resolve a divergence. Retirement is a status transition through the authority-state matrix, with a decision_registry record.

rules: commit_sha is required for E1/E2/E4 so the evidence is re-checkable at a fixed point in history.
locator identifies what within the file was observed: symbol name, line anchor, JSON pointer, or CSV row key. 'The file' is not a locator.
resolves_parity_id links the resolution to the exact baseline entry it retires, so the ledger and the data cannot drift apart.

disposition_classes.note: These are the values written into the baseline during PHASE-03 triage. The parity plan defined the enum; this plan defines what each one obligates.

classes.meaning: The registry record is wrong; the authority is right.
classes.obligation: Correct the record with E1/E2 evidence where available, else mark derived_from_authority_only at confidence medium. Remove the baseline entry in the same PR.

classes.meaning: Primary evidence supports the registry over the authority.
classes.obligation: Do NOT edit the record. Do NOT edit the authority. Open a governance issue, attach the evidence, keep the baseline entry with this disposition. Resolution moves to EAFIX-REGISTRY-CUTOVER-001 or a governance PR outside this plan.
classes.expected_volume: low but non-zero; a burn-down that finds zero stale authority almost certainly did not look

classes.meaning: The comparison itself is wrong: normalization, join key, or projection defect.
classes.obligation: Route to PHASE-02 as a tooling fix, not a data fix. Never correct data to compensate for a broken comparison.
classes.expected_volume: concentrated; usually a few defects producing many divergences

classes.meaning: Free prose or structurally incomparable field, deliberately accepted.
classes.obligation: Written rationale required. Counts against cutover for that registry unless the criteria in EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001 cutover_criteria are satisfied.

classes.meaning: NEW CLASS introduced by this plan. The divergence reflects a genuine disagreement between two modules in the running system, not a documentation error.
classes.obligation: Record the true, conflicting state in the registry with evidence from both sides. Open a code issue. Keep the baseline entry with this disposition. The registry's job is to record reality accurately, including when reality is broken.
classes.expected_volume: unknown; this is the class that justifies the entire registry system
classes.schema_change_note: This value must be added to the baseline schema's disposition enum. That is a schema change to the baseline file only, not to any registry record schema, and is permitted by this plan.

batching_and_pr_discipline.principle: Burn-down is many small, independently reviewable, independently revertible changes. Reviewability is the constraint, not throughput.
rules: One PR = one registry + one work class + at most 10 record changes.
Every PR must leave master green: build --check clean, parity --check clean, no new divergences, no stale baseline entries.
Every PR removes from the baseline exactly the parity_ids it resolved, and no others. Baseline shrinkage must equal the resolutions claimed.
Generated artifacts are regenerated in the same PR and reviewed alongside the authored change. An authored change whose generated diff was not read is not reviewed.
Never mix a tooling change and a data change in one PR. PHASE-02 adapter PRs and PHASE-04/05/06 data PRs are separate classes.
Never mix registries in one PR.
PR title: fix(registry): burn-down <registry> <work-class> batch <n>
Branch: fix/registry-burndown-<registry>-<work-class>-<n>
batching_and_pr_discipline.ordering_rule: Burn down in dependency order — module and process registries first, then contract and artifact, which reference them. Resolving a contract's producer_module_id against a module record that is itself about to change wastes the work and churns parity_ids.
batching_and_pr_discipline.stop_rule: If any batch produces an unexplained parity delta — divergences appearing or disappearing beyond the ones claimed — stop the batch, do not merge, and diagnose. Unexplained ledger movement means either the tooling or the understanding is wrong, and both are worse than a slow burn-down.

record_count_governance.problem: EAFIX-REGISTRY-POST-MERGE-REMEDIATION-001 PHASE-08 asserts required_results.records = 291 as an invariant. This plan will legitimately change that number: resolving an authority_only divergence means adding a record the registry was missing.
rules: Record count changes are permitted in this plan ONLY as the direct result of a dispositioned baseline entry.
Every PR that changes the record count must state: prior count, new count, delta, and the parity_id of each entry that justifies each added or retired record.
Records are never deleted. Retirement is a status transition, so retirement does not decrease the count.
PHASE-07 publishes the final count and the full add ledger.

The 291 constant in the remediation plan's required_results must be replaced with a reference to the current committed count rather than a hardcoded literal. A hardcoded count becomes a lie the moment the burn-down succeeds.
Amendment AMD-BURN-001 covers this: see predecessor_plan_amendments.

predecessor_plan_amendments.change: Replace the literal 291 with a committed expected-count file (EAFIX_auth_docs/01_canonical_registries/registry_expected_counts.json) that is updated deliberately in any PR that changes record counts, and validated as an exact match.
predecessor_plan_amendments.reason: A hardcoded record count is a correctness assertion that this plan is designed to invalidate. Making it a governed file preserves the fail-closed property (an unexpected count change still blocks) while allowing intentional change through review.

predecessor_plan_amendments.change: Remove the module.process_step_id foreign-reference exemption once FND-BURN-005 is resolved, restoring foreign_reference_errors = 0 as an unconditional blocking requirement.
predecessor_plan_amendments.reason: Temporary exemptions that outlive their cause become permanent holes. The exemption's removal is an explicit deliverable of this plan, not an assumption.

predecessor_plan_amendments.target: definition_of_finished: 'No stub records: every module record has responsibilities, process_step_ids, and confidence high.'
predecessor_plan_amendments.change: Amend to: 'No stub records: every module record has responsibilities and process_step_ids populated or an explicit planned status; confidence reflects the evidence class actually available per the evidence_protocol; zero records at confidence low; derived_from_authority_only records are enumerated and visible.'
predecessor_plan_amendments.reason: The roadmap criterion and the evidence protocol contradict each other. Facts asserted only by a catalog with no primary code or artifact backing are genuinely medium-confidence. Forcing confidence high would require either fabricating evidence or lying about the confidence field — and the confidence field exists precisely to carry this information. The evidence protocol wins; the roadmap criterion is corrected.

predecessor_plan_amendments.change: Add code_defect_referred to the disposition enum and to the baseline schema.
predecessor_plan_amendments.reason: The parity plan's disposition enum assumed every divergence is a documentation problem. Contract burn-down will surface divergences that are real integration defects in the running system. Those need a distinct, honest disposition rather than being mislabeled registry_defect_accepted.

phases.name: Dependency gate and work inventory snapshot

tasks.action: Verify both predecessor plans are merged and the system is green.
acceptance: All commands exit zero.
parity reports new_divergences=0, stale_baseline_entries=0.
registry_index.json unchanged: canonical_candidate, approved_for_cutover false.
tasks.fail_closed: If anything is red, stop. Do not begin editing data on an unproven substrate.

tasks.action: Snapshot the exact work inventory. This plan was authored without knowing these numbers; every count below is discovered, not assumed.
requirements: Total baseline entries, broken down by registry and by verdict.
contract_registry: total records, needs_review count, conflict-queue entries referencing contracts.
module_registry: total records, count with empty responsibilities, count with empty process_step_ids, count at each confidence level, full list of stub module_ids.
registry_conflict_queue.jsonl: total open entries.
Orphan report: the two orphan classifications with their current state.
AMD-REG-001 outcome: whether dangling module.process_step_id references were baselined, and how many.
Current total record count across all registries (confirm or correct the 291 figure).
outputs: BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md committed to the branch as the plan's own baseline of record
acceptance: Every number in this plan's confirmed_open_findings is either confirmed or corrected against actual data.
No estimate. Every figure is computed from committed files.

tasks.action: Land AMD-BURN-001 and AMD-BURN-004 as enabling changes before any burn-down batch.
requirements: Create registry_expected_counts.json from the snapshot and wire it into validation, replacing the hardcoded 291.
Add code_defect_referred to the baseline disposition enum and schema.
These are tooling/governance changes: one PR, no data changes.
acceptance: Validation still green.
An intentional count change updates the file; an unintentional one still blocks.

phases.name: Systematic divergence clustering (mechanical, no edits)
phases.rationale: Per-record triage of a large baseline is expensive and, done first, is mostly wasted. Divergences cluster. A field that diverges on nearly every joined record is one adapter or normalization defect wearing fifty costumes — not fifty data defects. Cluster first, so PHASE-02 can collapse the baseline with a handful of tooling fixes before any human reads a single record.

tasks.action: Implement a deterministic clustering report over the parity baseline.
method: Group every non-agree parity record by (registry, authority_path, field, verdict).
For each cluster compute: cluster size, joined-record population for that mapping, and divergence_rate = size / population.
Emit clusters sorted by size descending, deterministically.
requirements: Pure analysis. Reads the parity report. Writes one generated artifact. Modifies no authored data.
The clustering report is a managed generated output: rendered, hashed, --check covered, byte-stable. It is evidence and must be reproducible.
No fuzzy grouping. Exact key tuples only.

tasks.action: Apply systematic-defect heuristics to route clusters.

heuristics.pattern: field_divergence rate >= 0.80 on a single field
heuristics.route: PHASE-02 adapter review, NOT per-record triage
heuristics.reason: Eighty percent of records being individually wrong in the same field is far less likely than one comparison rule being wrong.

heuristics.pattern: authority_only rate ~1.00 AND registry_only rate ~1.00 on the same mapping
heuristics.inference: join key format mismatch — the two sides never matched at all (e.g. M-01 vs M01, path separators, case)
heuristics.reason: This produces a maximally alarming report from a trivial defect, and no amount of data editing would ever fix it.

heuristics.inference: genuine registry under-coverage — the registry really is missing records

heuristics.pattern: field_divergence rate low and scattered across records
registry_only concentrated in one module or subsystem
heuristics.inference: possible authority staleness for that subsystem, or registry over-claim
heuristics.route: PHASE-03 triage with elevated attention; strong candidate for authority_stale_suspected

rules: A heuristic ROUTES work. It never dispositions a record. Only PHASE-02 findings or PHASE-03 triage assign a disposition.
Heuristic thresholds are declared constants in the tooling, not judgement calls at review time.
A cluster routed to PHASE-02 that turns out NOT to be a tooling defect returns to PHASE-03 triage with that finding recorded.
outputs: Routing table: cluster -> suspected class -> destination phase

phases.name: Adapter and normalization corrections (tooling PRs)
phases.rationale: Fix the comparison before trusting what it reports. Every divergence this phase removes was never a data problem, and every hour spent triaging one would have been wasted.

tasks.action: For each PHASE-01 cluster routed as suspected systematic: prove or disprove the tooling defect by hand-checking at least 3 records in the cluster against primary sources.
requirements: Hand-check means: read the authority document, read the registry record, read the primary source, and state whether they actually disagree.
If they do not actually disagree, the comparison is defective. Proceed to fix.
If they do actually disagree, the cluster is genuine data divergence. Return it to PHASE-03 and record that the heuristic misrouted it.
acceptance: Every cluster has a written verdict: tooling_defect_confirmed or genuine_divergence_confirmed, with the hand-check evidence.

tasks.action: Correct confirmed adapter, normalization, and join-key defects in tools/registries/authority_projection.py.
requirements: One PR per adapter defect.
Add a regression test reproducing the defect using a synthetic fixture before fixing it.

Normalization rule changes are declared in parity_authority_map.json ONLY IF that mapping change is approved as a governance change — otherwise the fix belongs in the adapter's projection, not the map.

Never widen normalization to make things match. Widening normalization (aggressive case folding, punctuation stripping, whitespace obliteration) is copying by another name: it hides real divergence inside the comparison rule. Every normalization change must be justified as reflecting a genuine formatting equivalence, not a semantic one.
prohibited: Fuzzy matching, similarity thresholds, or any non-deterministic comparison (already prohibited by the parity plan; restated because this is the phase where the temptation peaks)
Normalizing away a difference that is actually meaningful

tasks.action: Re-derive the baseline for affected mappings only, under governed exception.
tasks.rationale: An adapter fix changes the comparison space, so parity_ids for that mapping may change wholesale. The parity plan forbids tooling from auto-appending to the baseline (PAR-051). This is the one sanctioned exception, and it is bounded.
requirements: Re-derivation is scoped to the single mapping the adapter fix touched. No other mapping's baseline entries may change in that PR.
The PR must report, per verdict, the before and after counts for that mapping.
The PR must state the expected direction and magnitude of the change BEFORE showing the result, and reviewers confirm the actual delta matches the explanation.
A re-derivation that increases divergence count is not automatically wrong — a fixed adapter may reveal real divergence a broken one was hiding — but it must be explained.
An adapter fix whose baseline delta cannot be explained is not merged.
acceptance: Baseline delta fully explained by the adapter change.
No unrelated mapping's entries touched.
parity --check green afterwards.

phases.name: Per-record triage and disposition (STEP-01; classification only, no edits)

tasks.action: Disposition every remaining baseline entry into exactly one class.
requirements: Classification requires investigation: locate primary evidence (E1/E2) where it exists before choosing a class.
A disposition of registry_defect_accepted chosen without looking for primary evidence is a guess wearing a label. The disposition record must state what was searched.
Every disposition is authored into registry_authority_parity_baseline.json with a written rationale field.
No registry record is edited in this phase. Classification only.
acceptance: unreviewed disposition count = 0
Every entry carries a rationale
Every entry states which evidence classes were searched and what was found
tasks.batching: Triage may be batched per registry. Triage PRs touch only the baseline file and are a distinct PR class from data PRs.

tasks.action: Open tracking issues from triage output.
requirements: One governance issue per authority_stale_suspected finding, with evidence attached.
One code issue per code_defect_referred finding, naming both modules and the conflicting interface claims.
One tracking issue per registry summarizing per-class counts, referenced from the burn-down PRs that follow.

tasks.action: Publish the triage summary and re-plan the remaining work against real numbers.
outputs: Batch plan: ordered list of PRs to execute in PHASE-04/05/06
E1/E2-available versus E3-only ratio, forecast per registry

decision_gate.condition: If the E3-only forecast exceeds a declared threshold (recommend 50%) for a registry
decision_gate.action: Stop and escalate before burn-down. That registry's facts are not independently evidenced anywhere, which means burn-down would largely transcribe the authority. That is a finding about the system worth surfacing to the owner, not a task to grind through. It may indicate the registry should be a derived projection of the authority rather than an independent source.

phases.name: Contract burn-down (STEP-02)
phases.rationale: Executed after module and process burn-down per the ordering rule, because contracts reference module and process IDs.

tasks.action: For each conflicted or needs_review contract record, establish the actual interface from primary evidence.
method: Locate the actual message/data structure in code or artifact: struct, schema, .mqh header, serializer, config contract.
Compare what the producer emits against what the consumer expects.
Record the finding before deciding the disposition.
branching_rule: Producer and consumer agree, registry record was wrong -> registry_defect_accepted. Correct the record with E1/E2 source_refs, clear needs_review, remove the baseline entry.

Producer and consumer genuinely disagree -> code_defect_referred. Record the true conflicting state in the registry with evidence from BOTH sides. Open a code issue. Keep the baseline entry with this disposition. Do NOT invent a reconciled interface that exists in neither module.
Neither side has locatable primary evidence -> resolve on E3 with confidence medium, tag derived_from_authority_only, and record that primary evidence was searched for and not found.
acceptance_per_batch: needs_review cleared only for records with recorded evidence

tasks.action: Reach contract_registry needs_review = 0 and baseline empty except code_defect_referred and authority_stale_suspected entries.
tasks.note: Contract registry cutover per EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001 cutover_criteria requires an EMPTY baseline. code_defect_referred entries therefore block contract cutover until the underlying code issues close. That is correct: a registry accurately recording a broken interface should not be promoted to authoritative-and-blocking while the interface is still broken. Surface this to the owner as a cutover dependency rather than resolving it here.
tasks.escalation: If code_defect_referred entries would indefinitely block contract cutover, that is a governance decision for the owner — either fix the code, or define a cutover exception class with recorded rationale. This plan does not make that call.

tasks.action: For each stub module identified in BURN-002, locate primary evidence for responsibilities and process_step_ids.
method: Use file_module_mapping.csv and the services reference to locate the module's implementation files.
Derive responsibilities from what the code actually does, corroborated by the module catalog.
Derive process_step_ids from actual call sites, handlers, and message flow, validated against process_registry IDs.
branching_rule: Implementation exists -> populate from E1 evidence, confidence high.

Implementation does not exist (planned module) -> set an explicit planned/not_implemented status rather than leaving fields empty. An empty field says 'we do not know'; a planned status says 'there is nothing to know yet'. These are different facts and the registry must distinguish them.
Implementation exists but responsibilities are only describable from the catalog -> populate at confidence medium, tagged derived_from_authority_only.
acceptance: Zero records with empty responsibilities that lack a planned status
Zero records at confidence low
needs_review_fields empty across module_registry
Every populated process_step_id resolves against process_registry (closes FND-BURN-005)

tasks.action: Remove the AMD-REG-001 foreign-reference exemption (AMD-BURN-002).
acceptance: foreign_reference_errors = 0 unconditionally and blocking
No exemption remains in the remediation plan's required_results

tasks.action: Disposition the two orphan classifications (including SK1_PLUGIN_INTERFACE and SK2_IDEMPOTENCY).
requirements: Each closure is an architectural judgement (E5) and therefore requires a decision_registry record with rationale.
Reclassification, retention as orphan-by-design, or retirement are all valid outcomes. Silence is not.
Retirement is a status transition, never a deletion.

tasks.action: Close every remaining entry in registry_conflict_queue.jsonl.
requirements: Every closure carries evidence or a decision_registry record.
An entry closed as 'no longer applicable' must state what changed to make it inapplicable.
The queue is emptied by resolution, never by truncation.
acceptance: registry_conflict_queue.jsonl has zero open entries
The 25 review items each map to a recorded closure

tasks.action: Full validation on a clean tree.

baseline_exit_state.contract_registry: 0 except entries dispositioned code_defect_referred or authority_stale_suspected, each with an open tracked issue

tasks.action: Honesty audit. This is the task that determines whether the burn-down was real.
checks: Ratio of resolutions carrying E1/E2 primary evidence versus E3-only, per registry. Publish it. A high E3-only ratio means the registry largely transcribes the authority and its cutover value is correspondingly reduced.
Count of authority_stale_suspected findings. Zero across a large burn-down is a red flag, not a success: it means either the authority is perfect or nobody looked at primary evidence.
Count of code_defect_referred findings. Publish with issue links.
Count of records tagged derived_from_authority_only. Publish per registry.
Confirm zero authority documents, routing files, doc_authority.json, registry_index.json, parity_authority_map.json, or schema files were modified.
Confirm every baseline removal has a corresponding resolves_parity_id in a committed record's source_refs.
outputs: BURNDOWN_EXIT_REPORT.md with all counts, the evidence-class ratio, and the delta against BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md
tasks.rule: The exit report states what was actually learned about the system, not merely that the numbers reached zero. A burn-down that produced no findings about the codebase did not investigate it.

tasks.action: Handoff.
outputs: Confirm STEP-01 through STEP-04 of the roadmap are complete.
Confirm which registries now satisfy EAFIX-REGISTRY-AUTHORITY-PARITY-001 cutover_criteria and which do not, with the blocking reason for each.
Open EAFIX-REGISTRY-COVERAGE-001 (STEP-05/06) with the unrepresentable and not_comparable inventory this plan deliberately left untouched.
Explicitly confirm no cutover was performed.

change: needs_review cleared per record with evidence
True conflicting state recorded for code_defect_referred cases
Explicit planned status for unimplemented modules
New decision records for orphan dispositions, retirements, and E5 judgements
Entries closed with recorded resolution; emptied by resolution only
Entries removed as resolved, one-for-one with committed evidence
Governed re-derivation for adapter-fixed mappings only (PHASE-02)
New governed file replacing the hardcoded 291 (AMD-BURN-001)
Adapter, normalization, and join-key defect fixes (PHASE-02 only)
Regression test per adapter defect fixed
Regenerated in every PR that changes authored data
NO CHANGE. Authority state is untouched by this plan.

acceptance_criteria: BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md exists and every count in this plan is confirmed or corrected against real data.
Clustering report is a deterministic, managed, --check-covered generated artifact.
Every confirmed adapter defect has a regression test and a bounded, explained baseline re-derivation.
Zero unreviewed dispositions in the baseline.
Every disposition carries a rationale and states which evidence classes were searched.
contract_registry needs_review = 0.
Zero module records with empty responsibilities lacking an explicit planned status; zero at confidence low.
Every module process_step_id resolves; foreign_reference_errors = 0 unconditionally and blocking.
registry_conflict_queue.jsonl empty; the 25 review items each map to a recorded closure.
Both orphan classifications dispositioned with decision_registry records.
Baseline empty for module, process, and artifact registries; contract baseline empty except tracked code_defect_referred and authority_stale_suspected entries.
Every baseline removal traces to a resolves_parity_id in a committed record's source_refs.
Zero records deleted.
Zero authority, routing, doc_authority, registry_index, parity_authority_map, or schema files modified.
Record count matches registry_expected_counts.json, and every count change is justified by an enumerated parity_id.
BURNDOWN_EXIT_REPORT.md publishes the E1/E2-versus-E3-only ratio, authority-staleness findings, and code-defect findings.
No authority cutover performed.
Registries remain canonical_candidate with approved_for_cutover false.
evidence_required: BURNDOWN_INVENTORY.md with the real starting numbers
Before/after baseline counts per adapter-fix PR, with the explanation stated before the result
One PR per batch, each with prior/new record counts and resolved parity_ids
Governance issue links for every authority_stale_suspected finding
Code issue links for every code_defect_referred finding
Full validation output on a clean tree
Explicit confirmation that no authority document was edited
Explicit confirmation that no cutover was performed
Per-registry cutover-readiness statement with blocking reasons

fail_closed_conditions: Either predecessor plan incomplete or master not green at PHASE-00
A batch produces an unexplained parity delta
Baseline shrinkage exceeds the resolutions claimed in a PR
A baseline entry removed without a corresponding resolves_parity_id in committed evidence
Any authority, routing, doc_authority, registry_index, parity_authority_map, or schema file appears in a burn-down diff
Any record deleted
Record count changes without an enumerated justifying parity_id
An adapter fix whose baseline delta cannot be explained
Normalization widened to make values match rather than to reflect genuine formatting equivalence
E3-only resolution ratio exceeds the declared threshold for a registry (escalate, do not grind)
Two consecutive builds produce a diff
CI cannot execute
response: Stop the batch. Do not merge.
Preserve logs, diffs, the parity report, and the clustering report.
Classify the cause as adapter logic, normalization rule, evidence misread, authority drift, genuine code defect, baseline hygiene, or environmental CI blockage.
Open a focused issue. Never widen this plan's scope to absorb a finding it was not authorized to fix.

specific_traps.name: Resolution by transcription
specific_traps.description: Copying authority values into registry records to clear the baseline.
specific_traps.rule: Prohibited without primary evidence. Where the authority is the only source, the record is tagged derived_from_authority_only at confidence medium and stays visible. The tag is the point: it tells a future reader exactly how much this record is worth.

specific_traps.name: Normalization laundering
specific_traps.description: Widening a normalization rule until two genuinely different values compare equal.
specific_traps.rule: Prohibited. Normalization encodes formatting equivalence, never semantic equivalence. If you are arguing that two values 'basically mean the same thing', you are writing a disposition, not a normalization rule.
specific_traps.detection: PHASE-02 hand-checks and adapter regression tests.

specific_traps.name: Inventing a reconciled interface
specific_traps.description: When producer and consumer genuinely disagree, writing a contract record describing a third interface that neither implements.
specific_traps.rule: Prohibited. Record the conflict as it exists. The registry's value is that it reports reality, including broken reality.
specific_traps.detection: Contract records whose source_refs cite only one side, or neither.

specific_traps.name: Scope absorption
specific_traps.description: Fixing schemas, adding mappings, or cutting over because the burn-down surfaced the need.
specific_traps.rule: Prohibited. Those are EAFIX-REGISTRY-COVERAGE-001 and EAFIX-REGISTRY-CUTOVER-001. Record the finding, hand it off, stay in scope.

specific_traps.name: Silent queue emptying
specific_traps.description: Closing conflict-queue entries as stale or not-applicable without stating what changed.
specific_traps.rule: Prohibited. A queue emptied without resolution is worse than a full queue, because it looks finished.

stages.gate_out: Baseline empty for module/process/artifact registries (contract baseline empty except tracked code_defect_referred); needs_review=0; conflict queue empty; orphans dispositioned; BURNDOWN_EXIT_REPORT.md published; registries remain canonical_candidate.

stages.title: Registry authority cutover and legacy supersession
stages.rationale: Every source plan excludes cutover (VF-04). A functioning SSOT requires the registries to become canonical and the legacy authorities to be superseded, exactly once, under human ratification.
stages.gate_in: STAGE-3 exit report accepted by owner.

tasks.action: Prepare the cutover brief: per registry, the parity result, record counts vs registry_expected_counts.json, remaining tracked exceptions (code_defect_referred), and the exact list of legacy documents to be superseded per parity_authority_map.json.
acceptance: Brief is generated from data, not authored prose; every superseded document enumerated with its replacement.

tasks.action: HD-10: owner ratifies cutover.
acceptance: Ratification recorded in decision_registry.jsonl with evidence refs to the brief and exit report.

tasks.action: Execute cutover in one PR: registry_index.json authority_status canonical / approved_for_cutover true per ratified registry; doc_authority.json marks legacy authorities superseded-by their registry; routing instructions route subjects to registries first.
acceptance: No legacy authority is both superseded and still routed as canonical for the same subject.
build --check, parity --check, and full test suite green after the change.
doc_authority.json path drift (stale EA-REG/ references, divergent copies) resolved in or before this PR so the supersession record itself has integrity.

tasks.action: Post-cutover honesty check: regenerate all managed artifacts on a clean tree; confirm two-build determinism; confirm no consumer (CI, tooling, docs) still reads a superseded authority as canonical.
acceptance: Zero references route to superseded authorities as canonical; determinism proven.

stages.gate_out: Registries are the canonical SSOT for module, process, contract, and artifact subjects; legacy authorities superseded; system green.

stages.title: Canonical operational process consolidation
stages.delegates_to_plan: EAFIX-PROCESS-CONSOLIDATION-SPEC v1.0.0 (normative detail: field superset section 4, record schema section 6, merge rules section 8, definition of complete section 11)
stages.gate_in: STAGE-4 complete for CON-P3 onward. CON-P1/CON-P2 may start after STAGE-1 (read-only).
merge_rules_summary: Never collapse required vs current behavior

tasks.title: Deterministic inventory
tasks.action: Enumerate all files in approved process-document paths; content-search for process/phase/step/flow/lifecycle/runbook/procedure/work-cell/state/branch/validation/failure/input/output/trigger/handoff; exclude archived paths by default; classify each candidate by domain, scope, authority, lifecycle status.
acceptance: No current process-related file omitted; every candidate classified.

tasks.title: Field extraction
tasks.action: For every inventoried document: extract headings/structured keys; map each source field to the canonical superset field (spec section 4); record unique values, duplicates, conflicts, source location and hash; mark unstructured facts for manual review.
acceptance: Every beneficial field mapped to a canonical destination or explicitly flagged.

tasks.title: Step and module reconciliation
tasks.action: For every canonical step: start from the post-cutover canonical process registry (successor to updated_trading_process_aligned.json and process_step_catalog.json); attach canonical module identity; add subsystem, manifest, work-cell, runtime, contract/channel/store/timing, validation/failure, and runbook detail; record conflicts and gaps.
acceptance: All 34 modules represented including U-series, SK-series, and cross-cutting modules; known process_step_catalog coverage gaps (P2_REPORTER, R3_CORRELATION_GUARD, U1-U4, SK1, SK2 absent; no step S27; 'Either' placeholder in output_contracts at line 670) surfaced as recorded conflicts, not silently patched.

tasks.title: Cross-cutting reconciliation
tasks.action: Reconcile once globally: time standard, identifier systems, checksum policy, idempotency, file sequence, atomic writes, transport hierarchy, retry policy, stale-data behavior, health/heartbeat, logging/traceability, risk-off default, operator override, security/permissions.
acceptance: Each cross-cutting policy has exactly one canonical statement; conflicts explicit.

tasks.title: Validation
tasks.action: Run the spec's required validations: unique process/phase/step IDs; valid module/channel/contract references; valid dependency graph and state transitions; no orphan inputs or undeclared outputs; every failure has behavior; every blocking gate and implementation claim has evidence; planned claims marked planned; conflicts visible; runbook and source references resolve; deterministic regeneration.
acceptance: All validations pass or produce explicit open-conflict records.

tasks.title: Ratification and projection generation (HD-11)
tasks.action: Owner ratifies the candidate; commit eafix_operational_process.current.json and eafix_operational_process.schema.json; generate EAFIX_OPERATIONAL_PROCESS.generated.md, process_flow.mmd/.svg, contract and failure matrices, implementation-gap report, and module/phase/operator views — all managed generated artifacts under build --check.
acceptance: Generated outputs reproduce byte-for-byte from the canonical record.

tasks.title: Supersession
tasks.action: Update doc_authority.json and routing; mark old end-to-end process narratives superseded; preserve Git history; keep subsystem runbooks as referenced procedures; forbid manual edits to projections; require process changes through governance.
acceptance: Old process authorities superseded; the JSON record is the sole process SSOT; the Markdown is a projection only.

stages.gate_out: Consolidation Definition of Complete (spec section 11) satisfied; eafix_operational_process.current.json is the single source of operational process truth.

stages.title: Migration governance: identities, naming, packaging, containers, path dispositions, generators
stages.delegates_to_plan: EAFIX-34-MODULE-REPOSITORY-MIGRATION-GAP-CLOSURE-V1 (workstreams WSP-WS6) and EAFIX-MODULE-FOLDER-NAMING-V1 (naming convention, validation, rename-map rules)
stages.gate_in: STAGE-4 complete (WS1 identity ratification uses the post-cutover canonical module registry as its authority source, satisfying the naming plan's precondition 3.1). STAGE-5 need not be complete, but its supersession list must be known before WS5 path dispositions are finalized.
stages.target_structure_input: EAFIX_PROPOSED_34_MODULE_FILE_TREE.md is illustrative input only (problem P-14); the executable target tree is generated in WS6 from ratified inputs and confirmed container inventories.

global_constraints.no_fabricated_ownership: No file, folder, schema, package, or contract may be assigned to a module without an evidence reference or an explicit human decision record.
global_constraints.one_identity_source: After ratification, all generators and validators must consume one current 34-module authority.
global_constraints.locator_permanence: Locators are assigned once, stored explicitly with module identity, never inferred at runtime, and never reused after retirement.
global_constraints.naming_governance_first: Folder generation must not proceed while the manifest separator or F4 symbol conflict remains open.
global_constraints.no_unsupported_empty_folders: The generated tree may contain only containers and nested folders approved by the module inventory and profile policy, except where a tool requires an empty directory and that exception is documented.
global_constraints.single_schema_authority: A contract shape has exactly one canonical definition. Module-local schema areas may contain references, projections, examples, or module-private schemas, but must not duplicate canonical cross-module schemas.
global_constraints.deterministic_generation: Same approved inputs must produce byte-identical trees, rename maps, inventories, and reports.
global_constraints.report_only_before_blocking: New structural validators begin in report-only mode and become blocking only after a clean baseline.
global_constraints.write_allowlists: Every implementation task must declare an explicit write allowlist and forbidden paths.
global_constraints.archive_immunity: Superseded/archive areas cannot be used as current authority and cannot be modified unless a task explicitly governs archival cleanup.
global_constraints.rollback_required: Every move or split must have an executable rollback entry and a pre-migration checkpoint.
global_constraints.branch_policy: Use an approved non-default branch; no direct push to master.

authored_artifacts.purpose: Governance-approved 34-module identity set and authority transition
Approved first-level containers instantiated by each module
Approved nested folder profiles by module/runtime type
Required and optional files at each module root
Ownership and governance of non-module repository roots
Canonical schema ownership, references, projections, and duplicate-prevention rules
Root, workspace, and per-module packaging decision
Human-approved dispositions for multi-module and unmapped files

generated_artifacts.purpose: One record for every tracked source path and its approved target disposition
References requiring updates across code, CI, packaging, docs, Docker, PowerShell, and MT4
Executable inverse map for migration rollback
Status and evidence report for multi-owner and unmapped paths
Inventory and destination status for non-module top-level paths

artifact_model.generated_file_rule: Generated artifacts must not be hand-edited.

required_profiles.applies_to: Bridge/transport modules spanning Python and MQL4
Modules containing distinct backend and frontend implementations

module_profile_model.profile_rule: Every module must select exactly one primary profile and may declare evidence-backed profile extensions. Folders not required by the selected profile or container inventory must not be generated.

validation_rules.description: Exactly 34 active canonical module records exist.
Module IDs, canonical symbols, and locators are unique.
Each module root matches locator plus kebab-case canonical symbol.
.module-id content matches the ratified module ID.
Manifest filename, manifest identity, folder identity, and .module-id agree.
Every first-level module container is approved in the container inventory.
Every nested folder is allowed by the selected module profile or extension.
Every required module-root artifact exists; prohibited artifacts do not exist.
Every tracked baseline path has an approved final disposition.
Every multi-owner or unmapped file has a human decision record.
Every move or split has an executable rollback entry.
Every retained global root complies with global_root_policy.json.
Every canonical schema has exactly one authority location.
Generated artifacts are current and byte-identical after regeneration.
No active authority or runtime path points to a retired legacy location.
No unsupported empty folder is committed.

tasks.title: Freeze repository baseline
steps: Record branch, commit SHA, tracked file list, symlinks, submodules, ignored generated paths, and current top-level tree.
Validate that the three source documents are present and record hashes.
Verify that the two naming-plan text files are identical.
acceptance_criteria: Every tracked path is present exactly once in the baseline inventory.
The duplicate-plan finding is deterministically confirmed.

tasks.title: Select the canonical naming-plan document
steps: Choose the surviving filename.
Mark the duplicate as superseded or delete it through documentation governance.
Update doc authority and routing only after the human decision.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one active naming-plan authority remains.
No routing or authority record points to the retired duplicate.

workstreams.title: Ratify module identities, symbols, and locators

tasks.title: Build the 27/28/34 identity crosswalk
steps: Extract module records from module_catalog.json, the readable module map, the vNext decisions, and all 34 manifests.
Produce one row per identity with current ID, symbols, aliases, status, source authority, and unresolved conflict flags.
acceptance_criteria: All 34 proposed module IDs appear exactly once.
Every old identity is mapped, retired, aliased, or explicitly unresolved.

tasks.title: Resolve F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR governance
steps: Locate or create the required RENAME_SYMBOL MCR.
Record F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR as a legacy alias if F4 is accepted.
Preserve numeric module ID 50000000000000000025.
acceptance_criteria: No active prefix+ordinal collision remains.
The accepted symbol is identical across identity authority, manifests, routing, crosswalks, and generated paths.

tasks.title: Ratify the 34-module authority
steps: Select and populate the current machine-readable identity authority.
Include module_id, canonical_symbol, status, module_locator, and legacy_aliases for every module.
Update routing and document authority to recognize the ratified source.
acceptance_criteria: Active module count equals 34.
IDs, symbols, and locators are each unique.
No locator is derived at validation time.
Older 25/27/28-module sources are explicitly classified as supporting, legacy, superseded, or partial.

workstreams.title: Resolve naming, packaging, and module-root artifact policy

tasks.title: Resolve manifest filename convention
steps: Apply HD-03.
Update the naming specification and all target-tree templates to the accepted separator.
Add a validator for manifest filename, module ID, canonical symbol, and .module-id agreement.
acceptance_criteria: One manifest filename convention is active.
All generated module paths use the same convention.

tasks.title: Choose packaging model
steps: Inventory current package/import/build behavior.
Evaluate root-only, per-module, and workspace packaging against runtime and deployment requirements.
Record the accepted model and migration consequences.
acceptance_criteria: The target tree contains pyproject.toml files only where the decision requires them.
Build, install, test, and import commands are defined for the selected model.

tasks.title: Define module-root artifact inventory
steps: Classify .module-id, manifest, README.md, AGENTS.md, pyproject.toml, and other root files as required, optional, generated, or prohibited by profile.
Define provenance and generation rules for README.md and AGENTS.md.
Forbid creation of empty generic context files without evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Every module has an explicit root-artifact set.
Every required artifact has an owner, source, and validation rule.

workstreams.title: Define containers and nested module profiles

tasks.title: Create module container inventory
steps: Derive current implementation needs from manifests, file mapping, runtime evidence, tests, and documentation.
Assign only first-level containers actually required by each module.
Record not_applicable reasons for omitted baseline containers.
acceptance_criteria: All 34 modules appear exactly once.
No unsupported container variant is used.
No empty folder is required solely for visual uniformity.

tasks.title: Create nested module profiles
steps: Ratify Python, MT4-native, mixed-runtime, UI full-stack, shared-kernel, and documentation-only profiles.
Assign exactly one primary profile to each module.
Record evidence-backed extensions for exceptional modules.
acceptance_criteria: Every proposed nested folder is authorized by a profile or explicit extension.
Runtime-constrained directory names remain unchanged.

workstreams.title: Govern global roots, schemas, state, and integration assets

tasks.title: Inventory and classify non-module top-level paths
steps: Inventory architecture, contracts, governance, integration-tests, tools, EAFIX_auth_docs, .github, .state, and all other top-level paths.
Classify each as canonical global root, migration candidate, compatibility root, generated root, runtime-only root, or archive.
acceptance_criteria: Every current and proposed non-module root is classified.

tasks.title: Approve global root policy
steps: Define purpose, owner, allowed content, prohibited content, naming, validation, and migration rules for each global root.
Define the relationship between integration-tests and module-local tests.
acceptance_criteria: No global root exists solely because it appeared in the proposed tree.
Every retained global root has an explicit authority and owner.

tasks.title: Approve schema placement policy
steps: Classify schemas as cross-module canonical, external-boundary canonical, module-private, projection, example, or compatibility schema.
Define reference direction and prohibit copied canonical definitions.
Define compatibility/versioning checks.
acceptance_criteria: Every schema file has exactly one canonical owner.
Duplicate canonical schema definitions equal zero.
Module-local schemas cannot silently override global contracts.

tasks.title: Define global .state contract
steps: Classify each state artifact as committed-generated, ignored-runtime, or prohibited.
Define regeneration, cleanup, and validation behavior.
Resolve whether any path index is permitted and ensure it does not become a second identity authority.
acceptance_criteria: Committed state is deterministic.
Runtime state is ignored and reproducible.
No permanent folder registry conflicts with the ratified identity authority.

workstreams.title: Map every repository path and resolve shared ownership

tasks.title: Generate initial file-destination matrix
steps: Join the tracked-file baseline with file_module_mapping.csv, module manifests, service references, test references, packaging metadata, and global-root policy.
Emit unresolved records rather than guessing.
acceptance_criteria: Every tracked source path appears exactly once as a source record.
No multi-owner file is silently assigned to one module.
No unmapped path is omitted.

tasks.title: Resolve multi-module service files
steps: Review calendar-ingestor files jointly mapped to D2, D3, F2, and D4.
Review reentry-engine files jointly mapped to E1, E2, and E4.
Review transport-router files jointly mapped to R2, O1, and B1.
For each file, approve move, split, shared compatibility layer, wrapper, or archive.
acceptance_criteria: Every multi-owner file has one approved disposition.
Every split has a split specification and behavioral equivalence test.

tasks.title: Resolve unmapped shared and UI files
steps: Review shared/idempotency, shared plugin, shared/reentry, shared/positioning, dashboard, GUI, desktop UI, and other unmapped paths.
Assign a module, retain globally, create a compatibility location, archive, or leave needs_review with an owner and deadline.
acceptance_criteria: Unmapped tracked paths equal zero before physical migration.
Every globally retained implementation file has a justified global owner.

tasks.title: Finalize destination and split specifications
steps: Populate target_path_or_paths, migration wave, rollback path, path consumers, and validation refs.
Freeze the matrix with a content hash.
acceptance_criteria: Every tracked path has a non-needs_review disposition.
Every move and split has an inverse rollback instruction.
The matrix hash is referenced by all generated migration artifacts.

workstreams.title: Build deterministic generators, validators, and impact analysis

tasks.title: Generate the authoritative target tree
steps: Generate module roots from the ratified identity authority and locator assignments.
Generate first-level containers from module_container_inventory.json.
Generate nested folders from module_folder_profiles.json.
Generate root artifacts from module_root_artifact_inventory.json.
Generate global roots from global_root_policy.json.
acceptance_criteria: The generated tree contains 34 unique module roots.
No hand-authored target-tree path exists without an approved source rule.
Repeated generation is byte-identical.

tasks.title: Generate rename, rollback, and impact maps
steps: Generate source-to-target operations from the frozen destination matrix.
Generate exact inverse rollback operations.
Scan source, tests, docs, workflows, Docker, PowerShell, packaging, MT4 includes, configuration, and scripts for path consumers.
acceptance_criteria: Every move has exactly one rollback operation.
Every path reference is classified as update_required, generated, runtime-only, false_positive, or needs_review.
Unresolved path consumers equal zero before each migration wave.

tasks.title: Implement structural validator
steps: Validate identity, locator, root name, .module-id, manifest filename, container inventory, profile structure, root artifacts, global roots, schema ownership, destination completeness, and duplicate paths.
Add report-only CI workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Validator can run on the pre-migration and proposed-tree states.
All findings have stable rule IDs and machine-readable output.

stages.gate_out: All HD-01..HD-09 decided and recorded; every tracked path has one approved disposition with evidence and rollback; target tree, rename map, impact report, rollback map, and validation report generated deterministically; migration wave gate criteria met.

stages.title: Physical migration waves and final enforcement
stages.gate_in: STAGE-6 gate_out satisfied, including the migration_wave_gate defined by the gap-closure plan.

required_before_each_wave: Zero needs_review path consumers in wave scope

tasks.title: Select pilots
selection_rules: One low-dependency Python module with single-owner files
One mixed-runtime or MT4-sensitive module only after the first two pass
acceptance_criteria: Pilot selection covers at least two profile types.
No pilot includes an unresolved multi-owner path.

tasks.title: Execute report-only pilot dry runs
steps: Apply operations in a temporary worktree or disposable branch.
Update path consumers.
Run structure, import, test, packaging, documentation-link, Docker, PowerShell, and MT4 checks as applicable.
Apply rollback and verify return to the exact baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Forward migration passes all applicable gates.
Rollback restores byte-identical tracked content and expected paths.
No hidden dependency remains unclassified.

tasks.title: Approve pilot results
acceptance_criteria: Pilot validation report is clean.
Any discovered rule changes are applied to authored inputs and generators, not patched into generated outputs.

tasks.title: Wave 1 — governance and documentation pointers
acceptance_criteria: Governance is internally consistent before code movement.

tasks.title: Wave 2 — single-owner Python modules
acceptance_criteria: Imports, tests, packaging, and service startup checks pass.

tasks.title: Wave 3 — UI and shared-kernel modules
acceptance_criteria: Backend/frontend, plugin, idempotency, and UI contract checks pass.

tasks.title: Wave 4 — split multi-module services
acceptance_criteria: Behavioral equivalence tests pass.
No compatibility wrapper is removed before all consumers migrate.

tasks.title: Wave 5 — MT4 and mixed-runtime modules
acceptance_criteria: MQL4 include paths, Experts/Indicators placement, terminal deployment scripts, and bridge integration tests pass.

tasks.title: Wave 6 — global roots and cleanup
acceptance_criteria: Global root policy passes.
No tracked source path remains at an unapproved legacy location.
Archives are not routed as active authority.

tasks.title: Run clean-clone verification
steps: Clone the migrated branch into a clean environment.
Regenerate all generated artifacts.
Run structural validation, all applicable tests, builds, packaging checks, docs-link checks, workflows, Docker checks, PowerShell checks, and MT4 checks.
Compare generated artifacts with committed generated outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Generated artifact drift equals zero.
Unmapped paths equal zero.
Unresolved ownership decisions equal zero.
Missing path references equal zero.
Duplicate module roots, IDs, locators, symbols, and canonical schemas equal zero.

tasks.title: Switch structural validation from report-only to blocking
acceptance_criteria: The clean baseline passes all blocking structural rules.
The workflow protects the default branch and pull requests.

tasks.title: Publish migration completion record
acceptance_criteria: Completion report references baseline SHA, final SHA, authority versions, decision records, artifact hashes, validation results, and rollback retention policy.

out_of_scope: Changing module responsibilities or contracts except where required to resolve identity or ownership conflicts
Adding, removing, merging, or splitting canonical modules outside an approved MCR
Refactoring business logic not required for file decomposition or path migration
Treating the proposed tree as authority before it is regenerated from approved inputs
Creating empty folders solely for visual symmetry
stages.gate_out: All 34 module roots follow the ratified convention; runtime and build behavior preserved and proven; rollback verified in pilot; CI enforces the final structure; gap-closure definition_of_done satisfied.

parallelization_allowances: STAGE-5 Phases CON-P1/CON-P2 (inventory, field extraction) may run in parallel with STAGE-3 because they are read-only. CON-P3 onward requires STAGE-4 complete.
STAGE-6 decision briefs for HD-01..HD-08 may be drafted during STAGE-3/4, but no decision is executed and no file moved before STAGE-4 exit.
Nothing in STAGE-7 starts before STAGE-6 exit.

registry_ssot: Registries canonical (not candidate); legacy authorities superseded; routing updated (STAGE-4).
Deterministic builds, complete --check coverage, blocking parity and integrity CI (STAGE-1/STAGE-2).
Zero needs_review, empty conflict queue, empty parity baseline except tracked code-defect referrals (STAGE-3).
process_ssot: eafix_operational_process.current.json is the sole process authority; projections byte-reproducible (STAGE-5).
physical_structure: One governance-recognized, populated 34-module identity authority exists.
All 34 permanent locator assignments are ratified.
F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR and manifest filename governance conflicts are closed.
The packaging model is recorded and implemented.
Every module has an approved container inventory, profile, and root-artifact inventory.
Every non-module root has an approved global-root policy.
Canonical schema ownership and module-local schema use are unambiguous.
Every tracked source path has one approved disposition with evidence and rollback.
Every multi-module or previously unmapped file has an approved resolution.
The target tree, rename map, impact report, rollback map, and validation report are generated deterministically.
Pilot migrations and rollback tests pass.
All migration waves pass applicable runtime, test, packaging, documentation, Docker, PowerShell, and MT4 checks.
Blocking CI prevents structural drift after migration.
governance: Every human decision HD-01..HD-11 recorded in decision_registry.jsonl with evidence.
Exactly one naming-plan authority survives (VF-01 resolved).
No document is both superseded and routed as canonical for the same subject.

required: State the stage and task IDs executed.
Report every gate check result with literal command outputs or generated-report paths as evidence.
Enumerate any deviation from this plan with rationale; deviations without recorded rationale are a blocking violation.
Never claim completion of a human-gated task; report it as awaiting the named HD decision.
